items.title: Gina Rinehart judgment looming in high-stakes battle over mining riches

children.content: Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart, expects a judgment to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.
Two old photographs of men, separated by a white line in the middle.
Red dirt at the side of the road with a sign that says 'Hope Downs mine 30km'

thumbnail.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.
Two old photographs of men, separated by a white line in the middle.
Red dirt at the side of the road with a sign that says 'Hope Downs mine 30km'

items.title: Dash cam video of near-misses on WA highways prompts road safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: Man on trial for alleged assault of several women

children.content: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

image.altText: A medium shot with a sign reading: District Court of Western Australia.

items.title: Perth GP suspended for helping patient use $18,500 in super on cosmetic lipo

children.content: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

image.altText: A doctor and nurse in an operating room in a hospital.

items.title: Cameron Green suffers IPL nightmare with bat and ball

children.content: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

image.altText: Cameron Green bowls in the IPL:

items.title: Four million litres of diesel secured by WA government 'won't touch the sides'

children.content: The diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state, but it has been dismissed as "woefully inadequate".

gallery.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.
Roger Cook speaks at a press conference with a serious expression.

thumbnail.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.
Roger Cook speaks at a press conference with a serious expression.

items.title: Travellers shocked by suspension of WA tourist flights

children.content: Rising fuel costs due to the war in Iran see Qantas suspend Jetstar flights between Sydney and Busselton.

image.altText: Passengers arrive at Busselton Airport after the flight from Sydney.

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

image.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

items.title: WA government eyes off unused COVID quarantine facility for prison

children.content: The WA government flags the possible conversion of the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre into a prison amid an "unprecedented" rise in the number of inmates being crammed into cells across the state.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a construction site

gallery.altText: Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

thumbnail.altText: Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

items.title: Teenager 'begging' minister to deport abuser who had visa reinstated

children.content: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

gallery.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

image.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: PM in Brunei as Chalmers warns of 'dangerous' time for economy

children.content: Anthony Albanese will leverage Australia's food exports while in Brunei in a bid to shore up long-term fuel supplies. Meanwhile, Treasurer Jim Chalmers is off to Washington following the release of the IMF's latest global economic outlook. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese presser

items.title: What a 'super El Niño' would mean for Australia's weather in 2026

children.content: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly thanks to the probable emergence of an incoming El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires are on the way.

articles.title: Flood watches issued as former Cyclone Maila brings days of heavy rain

children.content: Minor flood watches have been issued for several Far North Queensland catchments as former Tropical Cyclone Maila delivers sustained heavy rainfall, with forecasters warning transport routes could be disrupted.

image.altText: Man walking dog and drinking coffee on a beach

gallery.altText: A sunset reflecting on a lake with boats sailing and a table with seating in the foreground silhouetted.
A dirt road winds around a bend where smoke rises from burnt ground and trees.

thumbnail.altText: A sunset reflecting on a lake with boats sailing and a table with seating in the foreground silhouetted.
A flock of merino ewes with young lambs in a dusty paddock
A dirt road winds around a bend where smoke rises from burnt ground and trees.

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: IMF warns 'swift tightening' of interest rates may be needed to slow inflation

children.content: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

image.altText: A man walks along a rock wall with ocean and ships in the background.

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

image.altText: GFG Alliance executive chair Sanjeev Gupta.

items.title: ASX to follow Wall Street higher, oil prices sink on hope of further US-Iran peace talks

children.content: The Australian share market is likely to rise in morning trade as global investors bet on the US-Iran war being over within months, despite fuel shortages, higher inflation and a possible economic slowdown. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX board with stock names and prices blurred as board moves

items.title: Collard handed monster ban over 'entirely unacceptable' homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Trail mix and water help 87yo woman survive near-freezing night in dense forest

children.content: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

gallery.altText: A forest of giant karri trees, looking upwards through the trunks to the canopy
A sign in front of a road lined with trees.

thumbnail.altText: A forest of giant karri trees, looking upwards through the trunks to the canopy
A sign in front of a road lined with trees.

items.title: Should you turn off your laptop while commuting?

children.content: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

image.altText: A woman standing behind a desk wearing a loose button-up shirt reaches to shut down the lid of a laptop

items.title: Man who sent threatening letters was 'drinking himself to sleep'

children.content: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

image.altText: lakemba mosque

items.title: Massive hospital spend creates more questions than answers

children.content: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook wearing a white hard hat and yellow hi-ves vest speaks during a media conference at a construction site.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

image.altText: A woman in a dimly lit room screams

items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

children.content: A homeless survey conducted in one of WA's best-known holiday hotspots uncovers dozens of people sleeping rough, including a 75-year-old.

gallery.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Vans and cars parked at Surfer's point during the day

thumbnail.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Vans and cars parked at Surfer's point during the day

items.title: Quiz: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush

children.content: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

image.altText: Hong Kong street at sunset time

items.title: New lifesaving stroke prevention surgery arrives in Australia

children.content: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

image.altText: Woman smiling in a whote tshirt with garden in the background

items.title: Coalition's migration proposal faces practical questions

children.content: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

image.altText: Angus Taylor at a press conference in Parliament House.

items.title: Shock loss of Emily has friends saying 'get your moles checked'

children.content: Emily avoided the sun and "did everything right". No-one considered that a melanoma removed from her shoulder in 2022 had spread to her brain.

image.altText: A group of people in black and purple clothing sit together on a bench. Shirts read "Remembering Em."

items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

children.content: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.

gallery.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

thumbnail.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through
